evals.name: nodejs-pipeline-maintainable
evals.prompt: We're on self-managed GitLab 17.6 Ultimate at https://gitlab.acme-corp.internal. I need a .gitlab-ci.yml for our Node.js 20 service (monorepo root has pnpm-workspace.yaml, the service is in packages/api). Should install deps with pnpm, run eslint + vitest, build a Docker image pushed to our GitLab Container Registry, and deploy to a 'staging' environment on main. We have shared runners with docker executor tagged 'docker'. Make it maintainable — this is going to be copied across ~12 similar services by other teams.
evals.expected_output: A .gitlab-ci.yml that uses CI/CD components or a clean include structure, has named stages, uses default: block, rules instead of only/except, pinned image versions, interruptible jobs, and explicit guidance toward components given the version.

evals.name: pipeline-debug-image-not-found
evals.prompt:
my gitlab pipeline keeps failing on the build stage with 'ERROR: Job failed: Error response from daemon: pull access denied for registry.acme.internal/base/python, repository does not exist or may require authorization'. we're on gitlab 16.8 self-hosted. here's my job:

build:
  stage: build
  image: registry.acme.internal/base/python:3.11
  script:
    - pip install -r requirements.txt
    - pytest

whats wrong and how do I fix it
evals.expected_output: Should identify this as a registry auth issue for a private registry, explain that GitLab needs DOCKER_AUTH_CONFIG CI variable or runner-level config, link to the version-appropriate docs, and show a concrete fix.

evals.name: automation-list-my-mrs
evals.prompt: I'm on gitlab.com and I want a one-liner I can run from my terminal to list all open merge requests assigned to me across every project in my group 'acme-data'. I have Go installed. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Should recommend glab (because Go is available), walk through install via go install, auth, and give the actual command. Should NOT default to python-gitlab given Go is present.
